body: PT Darya Varia Laboratoria Tbk will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 48.16 billion, or IDR 43 per share, from its first-half 2024 profit, unchanged from last year despite a net profit increase to IDR 119.35 billion. The dividend will be disbursed on November 22, 2024, with shareholders listed on the recording date of November 4, 2023, eligible to receive the payment.
title: PT Darya Varia Laboratoria to Distribute IDR 48.16 Billion Interim Dividend from First Half 2024 Profit.

body: PT Waskita Karya Tbk has renewed a debt restructuring agreement worth IDR 26.21 trillion with its banking creditors, which includes a waiver of provisions related to cash management agreements valid until October 2026. The agreement involves a total outstanding principal amount of IDR 26.21 trillion, with credit facilities of IDR 24.15 trillion and sharia financing of IDR 2.05 trillion, and is expected to be finalized after reconciliation with all creditors.
title: PT Waskita Karya renews debt restructuring agreement with creditors worth IDR 26.21 trillion.

body: PT Waskita Beton Precast Tbk reported a loss of IDR 640.6 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 25.6% increase year-on-year, despite a 29% rise in operating revenues to IDR 1.33 trillion. The company's costs, including revenue costs, sales expenses, and financial expenses, increased significantly, leading to the loss, while its parent company, PT Waskita Karya Tbk, received a lower credit rating due to incomplete debt restructuring.
title: PT Waskita Beton Precast reports 640.6 billion IDR loss in first nine months of 2024, a 25.6% year-over-year increase.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) fell 7,739.15 at 10:05 WIB, weakening to 7,762.39 by 11:18 WIB, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, and IDXESG also experiencing corrections ranging from 0.03% to 0.56%. The decline was influenced by share prices of major issuers in the banking and energy industries, including PT Bank Central Asia TBK and PT Adaro Energy Indonesia TBK, while shares of companies like PT Barito Renewable Energy TBK and PT Astra International TBK showed strengthening.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Slows Down at Start of Trading Session

body: Indonesia's President Prabowo Subianto and Vice President Gibran Rakabuming Raka have inaugurated their cabinet for the 2024-2029 period, comprising 17 ministers and deputy ministers, including key appointments such as Teuku Riefky Harsya as Minister of Creative Economy and Rosan Roeslani as Minister of Investment Downstreaming. The new cabinet aims to drive economic growth and development in various sectors, including creative economy, investment, environment, and agrarian affairs.
title: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto Inaugurates New Cabinet Members for 2024-2029 Period

body: PT Ashmore Asset Management Indonesia Tbk will distribute a final dividend of IDR 27.5 per share, totaling IDR 60.81 billion, following a general meeting of shareholders on October 17, 2024, where they agreed to distribute cash dividends for the 2023 financial year. The company posted a net profit of IDR 106.78 billion in 2023, with the remainder designated as retained earnings.
title: PT Ashmore Asset Management Indonesia to Distribute IDR 60.81 Billion in Final Dividends to Shareholders

body: The Indonesian Stock Exchange (IDX) recorded its 10 most expensive stocks on Friday, with prices ranging from IDR 14,000 to IDR 46,000 per share, representing various industries such as IT, heavy equipment, property, and more. These high-priced stocks are actively traded on the market, with their prices reflecting their strong performance and market demand.
title: PT Bursa Efek Indonesia Lists 10 Most Expensive Stocks Across Various Industries

body: PT Erajaya Swasembada Tbk's subsidiary, PT Sinar Eka Selaras Tbk, has injected IDR 15.73 billion into PT MGI Golf Indonesia to maintain its 49% stake in the joint venture, which focuses on retailing golf products. The capital injection will increase MGI's issued and paid-up capital to IDR 78.45 billion from IDR 62.72 billion, with Eral's total assets and equity standing at IDR 2.06 trillion and IDR 1.50 trillion, respectively, as of June 30, 2024.
title: Erajaya Swasembada's subsidiary invests IDR 15.73 billion in PT MGI to maintain 49% stake.

body: PT Adaro Energy Indonesia's shareholders have approved a plan to divest shares in PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia, aiming to generate approximately 50% of revenue from non-thermal coal segments by 2030. The company targets to record over 50% of its revenue from non-thermal coal business by 2030, driven by increasing business potentials in segments that favor a green ecosystem in Indonesia.
title: Adaro Energy to diversify business beyond thermal coal, aiming for 50% non-coal revenue by 2030.

body: PT Esta Indonesia Tbk, which debuted on the Indonesia Stock Exchange in August 2024, has acquired 6 plots of land and buildings from its President Director, Hoo Anton Siswanto, for IDR 12.3 billion. The company had raised IDR 164.5 billion from its initial public offering (IPO) in August, issuing 822.5 million shares at IDR 200 per share, representing 20% of its issued and paid-up capital.
title: PT Esta Indonesia acquires 6 plots of land and building from its President Director for IDR 12.3 billion in an affiliate transaction.

body: PT Samuel International will acquire 216.32 million shares of PT MD Entertainment Tbk through a private placement, increasing the company's shares to 9.72 billion from 9.51 billion, with an exercise price of IDR 3,060 per share. This acquisition will raise PT Samuel International's stake in PT MD Entertainment Tbk to 2.22% of total shares issued, with a total value of IDR 661.94 billion.
title: PT Samuel International to Acquire 216 Million Shares of PT MD Entertainment via Private Placement.

body: Kasikornbank (KBank) has acquired 84.55% of PT Bank Maspion Tbk, injecting IDR 3.5 trillion in capital, and plans to utilize its expertise in MSME financing and digital banking to drive Bank Maspion's performance in Indonesia. KBank's financial report shows a net interest income of THB 76 billion (IDR 35.5 trillion) and a net profit of THB 26.2 billion (IDR 12.2 trillion), with a goal to make Bank Maspion the largest bank in East Java by 2027.
title: Kasikornbank Acquires 84.55% Stake in Indonesia's Bank Maspion to Expand MSME Financing and Digital Banking.

body: Indonesia's MSMes are urged to make a global-scale breakthrough, with the Indonesian embassy in Thailand facilitating market access through exhibitions and programs, but stakeholders must work together to support MSME growth, with only 20.5% of total credits in Indonesia going to MSMes. Meanwhile, Kasikornbank, a leading Thai bank, is set to enter Indonesia through its acquisition of PT Bank Maspion Tbk, aiming to contribute to financial inclusion and MSME financing, with MSMes accounting for over 61% of Indonesia's GDP.
title: Indonesian Ambassador to Thailand Urges MSMES to Improve Quality to Conquer Global Market

body: Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison's 1:4 stock split has quadrupled the number of shares in circulation, with major shareholders such as Ooredoo Hutchison Asia Pte. Ltd. and PT Tiga Telekomunikasi Indonesia increasing their holdings to 21.17 billion and 2.01 billion shares, respectively. Meanwhile, Qatar National Bank reduced its ownership in PT Bank QNB Indonesia TBK from 95.63% to 91.57%, and other investors made notable changes to their shareholdings in various companies, including PT Indosat TBK, PT Mark Dynamics Indonesia TBK, and PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK.
title: Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison undergoes stock split, leading to changes in share ownership among major investors.

body: Individual investors, including Medya Lengkey and Yulisar Khiat, increased their stakes in various companies, such as PT Metrodata Electronics TBK and PT Medikaloka Hermina TBK, with ownership percentages ranging from 12.59% to 22.16%. Meanwhile, other investors, including PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama and PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala, sold shares in companies like PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK and PT Sinar Mas Multiartha TBK, resulting in changes to their ownership stakes.
title: Individual investors and companies in Indonesia make notable changes to their shareholdings in various listed companies.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 1.51 billion shares, with the largest buys in digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (229.41 million shares) and mineral mining company PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (706.43 million shares). Meanwhile, the top-selling shares included e-commerce company PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (-110.89 million shares) and banking industry PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (-38.38 million shares).
title: Foreign investors purchase 1.51 billion shares surplus in Indonesian market, led by digital company GoTo and mining firms.

body: PT Indomobil Multi Jasa TBK, a consumer financing company, reported revenue of IDR 2.71 trillion for the first nine months of 2024, a 7.3% year-over-year increase, with vehicle lease and financial services contributing IDR 1.65 trillion and IDR 1.06 trillion, respectively. However, the company's net profit for the same period slowed down to IDR 105.95 billion, a decline from IDR 124.63 billion in the same period last year, due to increased general administrative expenses and a decrease in profit share from associates.
title: Indomobil Multi Jasa's revenue increases 7.3% to IDR 2.71 trillion in first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Central Omega Resources Tbk has acquired 100% of PT Bumi Bintang Silika, a limestone mining company, for IDR 100 million through its subsidiaries, as part of its long-term strategy to expand its business activities in Indonesia. The acquisition has positively impacted the company's stock price, which rose 1.46% today and 21.93% since the beginning of the year, according to IDNFinancials data.
title: Central Omega Resources acquires 100% of limestone mining company Bumi Bintang Silika through its subsidiaries.

body: PT Tancorp Investama Mulia increased its stake in PT Superior Prima Sukses Tbk to 10.256% from 9.505% through a transaction that added 911.8 million shares, with a total of 66.8 million shares acquired at a price range of IDR 220-234 per share. As of September 30, 2024, the company's total shares stood at 8.89 billion, with a free float rate of 16.26% and 6,091 shareholders.
title: PT Tancorp Investama Mulia increases stake in PT Superior Prima Sukses TBK to 10.256%.

body: PT Golden Eagle Energy Tbk is issuing 315 million shares through a private placement to raise additional working capital, which will increase its issued and paid-up capital to IDR 433.12 billion and boost its asset equity by approximately IDR 39.37 billion. The company's total assets are projected to rise to IDR 1.18 trillion, with liabilities remaining at IDR 304.79 billion and total equity increasing to IDR 878.55 billion.
title: PT Golden Eagle Energy to Issue 315 Million Shares in Private Placement to Increase Working Capital
